text.title: Blended CAC Model Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Blended CAC Model application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Apply to a single paid-spend window (campaign, month, or quarter). Do not batch multiple windows in one record.
rule.rationale: Each window has its own attribution decisions; batching destroys reproducibility.

rule.statement: Every channel row carries spend (USD), customers acquired (int), source path. Free-form prose without typed values is rejected.
rule.rationale: Typed inputs make the readout falsifiable; untyped values hide hallucinations.

rule.statement: Output names a single accountable owner. No 'growth team' or 'we' as owner.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership equals none; future readers need a named human.

rule.statement: Output carries semver + last_reviewed date. Re-run on material spend changes; do not silently overwrite.
rule.rationale: Blended CAC drifts with channel mix; old records must be flagged.

rule.statement: Attribution window (e.g. 7-day click, 1-day view) is declared up front and frozen for the duration of the test.
Subtract platform-claimed conversions by an incremental-lift estimate (geo-test, holdout, or default 30% haircut documented in the record).
rule.rationale: Without lift adjustment, platform CAC double-counts across channels.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
